chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Along with that we can also do indexing with our arrays. Indexing is something that we have seen in the list.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Indexing basically means that how we can access values by going to a particular index in our array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: To access values we can take example of a very simple array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Let's suppose here we have created a very basic array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: numpy.array

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: which is a 1D array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Now we have to index in this array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: The process is same as python list.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We know that first value is index 0, then index 1, 2, 3, 4.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: This is how our index values work.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We can print them in the same way.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We can access array of 0.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: In the same way we can access array of 3.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We will get value 1 on index 0.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: This is index 1, this is index 2, this is index 3.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So the value on index 3 will be equal to 4.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We will get 1 and 4 values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So this is indexing on a normal plain 1D array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We can do the same indexing on 2D arrays.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For 2D arrays let's copy our array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: In fact let's keep this and comment it out.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: To index on 2D arrays we specify our row and column values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For example, let's take an example of this array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We know that this is row 1.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Row 1 is basically indicated with index 0.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: This is row 2.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Similarly this is column 0, this is column 1, this is column 2.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We see row horizontally and columns vertically.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: To access any specific value of an array,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: let's suppose we want to access this 5 value.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For this we have to define row and column.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Row is 1 and column is 1.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So this will be array of 1,1.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Similarly if we want to access 3,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: then row is 0 and column is 2.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So this is 0,2.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So we have our array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: If we want we can print array of 1,1.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We will get 5.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: And if we want we can print 0,2.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So in 0th row,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: the second index element which is going to be 3 will be printed.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Here we have talked about normal indexing.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: But along with normal indexing there is also something which is called fancy indexing.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Now what exactly is this fancy indexing?

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Fancy indexing means that we can access a particular set of indexes together.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For example,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: we have this array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: It has values 0,1,2,3,4,5.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Now we want to access a set of values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We want to access values of index 0, 1 and 3 together.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For that we can use fancy indexing.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: How?

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: First of all we can define the index values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We want to access values of 0,1 and 3.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We don't have to do anything.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We just have to write array of these index values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: As soon as we enter these values,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: we have 1 on 0, 2 on 1st index and 4 on 3rd index.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: And these values will be printed.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So fancy indexing can be done easily in our NumPy arrays.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Why? Because we have vectorization.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So basically NumPy sees the whole array as a whole rather than traversing it one by one.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: And we can't do this easily in Python list.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So this is called fancy indexing.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Along with fancy indexing, we also have boolean indexing.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Boolean indexing means that depending upon a particular condition,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: that the condition value for which the index value is true,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: that should be included in our answer.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Based on a particular condition, we can filter out our values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: For example, we have these values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: If we talk about boolean indexing, we have these values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: And let's suppose we want to filter out values greater than 3 or greater than 2 from this array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: So we can filter out those values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: We can simply write array of and we can write our boolean value instead of index.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Boolean value is array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: The variable name we have given to our array.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: If the index value of the array is greater than 3,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: all those values will be printed.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: If the values are greater than 2, all those values will be printed.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: If let's suppose we want to print all even values,

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: we can write a proper condition here.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: a modulo 2 equal to 0.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: All even values will be printed.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: Similarly, we can print odd values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: not equal to 0.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: These are all the odd values.

chunks.title: Indexing
chunks.text: This is how we use boolean indexing.

Indexing basically means that how we can access values by going to a particular index in our array. To access values we can take example of a very simple array. We will get value 1 on index 0. This is index 1, this is index 2, this is index 3. So the value on index 3 will be equal to 4. We will get 1 and 4 values. So this is indexing on a normal plain 1D array. We can do the same indexing on 2D arrays. For 2D arrays let's copy our array. In fact let's keep this and comment it out. To index on 2D arrays we specify our row and column values. For example, let's take an example of this array. We know that this is row 1. Row 1 is basically indicated with index 0. This is row 2. Similarly this is column 0, this is column 1, this is column 2. We see row horizontally and columns vertically. To access any specific value of an array, let's suppose we want to access this 5 value. For this we have to define row and column. Row is 1 and column is 1. So this will be array of 1,1. Similarly if we want to access 3, then row is 0 and column is 2. So this is 0,2. So we have our array. If we want we can print array of 1,1. We will get 5. And if we want we can print 0,2. So in 0th row, the second index element which is going to be 3 will be printed. Here we have talked about normal indexing. But along with normal indexing there is also something which is called fancy indexing. Now what exactly is this fancy indexing? Fancy indexing means that we can access a particular set of indexes together. For example, we have this array. It has values 0,1,2,3,4,5. Now we want to access a set of values. We want to access values of index 0, 1 and 3 together. For that we can use fancy indexing. How? First of all we can define the index values. We want to access values of 0,1 and 3. We don't have to do anything. We just have to write array of these index values. As soon as we enter these values, we have 1 on 0, 2 on 1st index and 4 on 3rd index. And these values will be printed. So fancy indexing can be done easily in our NumPy arrays. Why? Because we have vectorization. So basically NumPy sees the whole array as a whole rather than traversing it one by one. And we can't do this easily in Python list. So this is called fancy indexing. Along with fancy indexing, we also have boolean indexing. Boolean indexing means that depending upon a particular condition, that the condition value for which the index value is true, that should be included in our answer. Based on a particular condition, we can filter out our values. For example, we have these values. If we talk about boolean indexing, we have these values. And let's suppose we want to filter out values greater than 3 or greater than 2 from this array. So we can filter out those values. We can simply write array of and we can write our boolean value instead of index. Boolean value is array. The variable name we have given to our array. If the index value of the array is greater than 3, all those values will be printed. If the values are greater than 2, all those values will be printed. If let's suppose we want to print all even values, we can write a proper condition here. a modulo 2 equal to 0. All even values will be printed. Similarly, we can print odd values. not equal to 0. These are all the odd values. This is how we use boolean indexing.
